title: Journey Through Ancient India
subtitle: one road across five ages
goalMeaning: Each arm of the board is an age of India — from the Indus cities to the Mughal courts. Carry your travellers through all of them and home.

enter.text: A traveller sets out along the great trunk road that has carried India's people for five thousand years.
From the still centre the caravan departs. Every age of India lies ahead, waiting to be crossed.
The cowries decide the weather of your road; your wits decide the route.

castle.text: A caravanserai — a walled rest-house on the trade road, where travellers of every faith slept safe.
Here at a river crossing or temple town, no bandit may touch you. Trade, learn, and move on.
The old roads were dotted with sanctuaries. Rest, and let the season of danger pass.

capture.text: You overtake a rival caravan and send it back. Fortunes on the trade road were won and lost like this.
Empires rose by displacing those who came before. Your gain is another's long road back.
The market rewards the bold — but the wheel of commerce turns, and today's victor is tomorrow's debtor.

captured.text: Monsoon, bandits, or ill luck have sent your traveller back to the start. The road has always been perilous.
A caravan scattered can be gathered again. Patience was the first virtue of the long-distance trader.
Even mighty cities fell and were rebuilt. Begin your crossing anew.

home.text: A traveller completes the crossing of all five ages and comes home laden with story and trade.
From the Indus to the Mughal court and back to the centre — one whole journey through time.
One caravan is safely home. The others still wind through the ages.

win.text: All four travellers are home. You have carried them across the whole living history of India and back.
The journey through the ages is complete — Indus, Maurya, Gupta, Vijayanagara, Mughal, and home.

journey.text: The Indus Valley: you set out from the world's first great planned cities, Harappa and Mohenjo-daro.
The Mauryan age: Ashoka's edicts line the road, carved in stone and counselling non-violence.
The Gupta age: the road runs bright with mathematics, astronomy, and the zero that changed the world.
Vijayanagara: you pass a southern empire of temple-cities, bazaars, and diamond markets.
The Mughal age: the homeward road climbs past gardens and the great pachisi court of Fatehpur Sikri.
